class_0: Urban fabric: areas covered by buildings, roads, and infrastructure.
Continuous urban fabric: Densely built city areas with a high proportion of sealed surfaces.
class_1: Industrial or commercial units: areas occupied by factories or commercial buildings.
Discontinuous urban fabric: Suburban or peri-urban built-up areas mixed with vegetation and open spaces.
class_2: Arable land: land used for crop cultivation.
Industrial or commercial units: Areas dominated by industrial plants, warehouses, logistics, or commercial facilities.
class_3: Road and rail networks and associated land: Transport corridors and related infrastructure such as roads, railways, yards, and verges.
Road and rail networks and associated la: Transport corridors and related infrastructure such as roads, railways, yards, and verges.
class_4: Port areas: Harbor and dock infrastructure for maritime transport and logistics.
Port areas: Harbor and dock infrastructure for maritime transport and logistics.
class_5: Pastures: grassland areas used for grazing livestock.
Airports: Airport runways, taxiways, terminals, and associated aviation infrastructure.
class_6: Mineral extraction sites: Open-pit mines, quarries, and extraction areas for mineral resources.
Mineral extraction sites: Open-pit mines, quarries, and extraction areas for mineral resources.
class_7: Dump sites: Waste disposal and landfill areas used for dumping solid material.
Dump sites: Waste disposal and landfill areas used for dumping solid material.
class_8: Construction sites: Active construction zones with exposed soil and temporary built structures.
Construction sites: Active construction zones with exposed soil and temporary built structures.
class_9: Coniferous forest: areas dominated by evergreen needle-leaf trees.
Green urban areas: Urban parks, public gardens, and vegetated open spaces within settlements.
class_10: Sport and leisure facilities: Sports grounds, stadiums, golf courses, and recreational complexes.
Sport and leisure facilities: Sports grounds, stadiums, golf courses, and recreational complexes.
class_11: Non-irrigated arable land: Cropland mainly relying on rainfall rather than irrigation systems.
Non-irrigated arable land: Cropland mainly relying on rainfall rather than irrigation systems.
class_12: Permanently irrigated land: Cropland regularly supplied with water through irrigation infrastructure.
Permanently irrigated land: Cropland regularly supplied with water through irrigation infrastructure.
class_13: Rice fields: Paddy fields used for rice cultivation, often seasonally flooded.
Rice fields: Paddy fields used for rice cultivation, often seasonally flooded.
class_14: Vineyards: Agricultural land planted with grapevines, usually in row patterns.
Vineyards: Agricultural land planted with grapevines, usually in row patterns.
class_15: Fruit trees and berry plantations: Orchards and plantations for fruit and berry production.
Fruit trees and berry plantations: Orchards and plantations for fruit and berry production.
class_16: Olive groves: Agricultural land dominated by olive tree cultivation.
Olive groves: Agricultural land dominated by olive tree cultivation.
class_17: Pastures: Grass-dominated agricultural land used primarily for livestock grazing.
Pastures: Grass-dominated agricultural land used primarily for livestock grazing.
class_18: Annual crops associated with permanent crops: Mosaic agricultural areas combining annual crops with orchards or other permanent crops.
Annual crops associated with permanent c: Mosaic agricultural areas combining annual crops with orchards or other permanent crops.
class_19: Complex cultivation patterns: Small-scale mixed farming parcels with heterogeneous crop patterns.
Complex cultivation patterns: Small-scale mixed farming parcels with heterogeneous crop patterns.
class_20: Mixed forest: areas with a mixture of coniferous and broad-leaved trees.
Land principally occupied by agriculture: Agricultural land interspersed with substantial natural vegetation patches.
class_21: Agro-forestry areas: Land where trees and agricultural production are deliberately combined.
Agro-forestry areas: Land where trees and agricultural production are deliberately combined.
class_22: Broad-leaved forest: forests dominated by deciduous trees.
Broad-leaved forest: Forest stands dominated by deciduous broad-leaf tree species.
class_23: Coniferous forest: Forest stands dominated by evergreen needle-leaf tree species.
Coniferous forest: Forest stands dominated by evergreen needle-leaf tree species.
class_24: Mixed forest: Forest stands containing both broad-leaved and coniferous tree species.
Mixed forest: Forest stands containing both broad-leaved and coniferous tree species.
class_25: Transitional woodland: areas between forest and shrubland.
Natural grassland: Natural herbaceous vegetation dominated by grasses with limited tree cover.
class_26: Moors and heathland: Shrub and heath vegetation, often on nutrient-poor and acidic soils.
Moors and heathland: Shrub and heath vegetation, often on nutrient-poor and acidic soils.
class_27: Water bodies: lakes, rivers, or reservoirs.
Sclerophyllous vegetation: Hard-leaved evergreen shrub or woodland vegetation adapted to dry climates.
Transitional woodland/shrub: Successional areas between shrubland and forest, often regenerating woodland.
class_29: Beaches, dunes, sands: Unconsolidated sandy coastal or inland landforms including dunes.
Beaches, dunes, sands: Unconsolidated sandy coastal or inland landforms including dunes.
class_30: Bare rock: Exposed bedrock or rocky surfaces with very sparse vegetation.
Bare rock: Exposed bedrock or rocky surfaces with very sparse vegetation.
class_31: Sparsely vegetated areas: Land with low vegetation cover and substantial exposed ground.
Sparsely vegetated areas: Land with low vegetation cover and substantial exposed ground.
class_32: Burnt areas: Land recently affected by wildfire or other burning events.
Burnt areas: Land recently affected by wildfire or other burning events.
class_33: Inland marshes: Freshwater marsh wetlands located away from coastal influence.
Inland marshes: Freshwater marsh wetlands located away from coastal influence.
class_34: Peatbogs: Wetland ecosystems with peat accumulation and waterlogged organic soils.
Peatbogs: Wetland ecosystems with peat accumulation and waterlogged organic soils.
class_35: Salt marshes: Coastal wetlands regularly influenced by saline tidal waters.
Salt marshes: Coastal wetlands regularly influenced by saline tidal waters.
class_36: Salines: Salt production basins or natural saline flats with high salt concentration.
Salines: Salt production basins or natural saline flats with high salt concentration.
class_37: Intertidal flats: Tidal mudflats or sandflats exposed at low tide and submerged at high tide.
Intertidal flats: Tidal mudflats or sandflats exposed at low tide and submerged at high tide.
class_38: Water courses: Linear inland water features such as rivers, streams, and canals.
Water courses: Linear inland water features such as rivers, streams, and canals.
class_39: Wetlands: areas saturated with water, marshes or swamps.
Water bodies: Standing inland waters such as lakes, reservoirs, and ponds.
class_40: Coastal lagoons: Shallow coastal water bodies partially separated from the sea.
Coastal lagoons: Shallow coastal water bodies partially separated from the sea.
class_41: Estuaries: Transitional coastal zones where river water mixes with seawater.
Estuaries: Transitional coastal zones where river water mixes with seawater.
class_42: Sea and ocean: Open marine waters beyond inland and estuarine systems.
Sea and ocean: Open marine waters beyond inland and estuarine systems.
